page: Amazon Web Services r8idb.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by Intel processors with 96 virtual CPUs and 48 physical cores, supporting two threads per core. The dedicated CPUs operate at a baseline speed of 3.9 GHz.

This instance is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. For local storage, the configuration includes 5700 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes, providing high-speed local data access directly attached to the hypervisor.

Networking capabilities feature a baseline bandwidth of 100 Gbps. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the r8idb.24xlarge offers high resource density by combining large memory capacity with substantial local NVMe storage, reducing the need for external network-attached storage for data-intensive operations. This hardware profile is designed for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale databases, and real-time data processing applications that require high-throughput local storage and high network bandwidth.
description: Amazon Web Services r8idb.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by Intel x86_64 processors running at 3.9 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this server provides dedicated CPU allocation and 768.0 GB of system memory, establishing an 8.0 GB memory-to-core ratio. It includes 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes for high-speed local storage, alongside a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. The combination of high memory capacity, fast local NVMe storage, and high-speed networking makes this instance suitable for memory-intensive databases, large-scale data processing, and enterprise workloads requiring low-latency local storage access without relying solely on network-attached storage.
